MINI MINUTES:

Mr. Sahae made reference to the March 14, 2016 order granting prejudgment interest and the March 11, 2016 stipulation regarding costs. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. In re: PLAINTIFF CAROL GILBERT, INC'S Motion For Attorney Fees - Plaintiff Carol Gilbert, Inc. is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $98,933.82. All of the arguments made by Parking Corp. to reduce the amount of fees lack merit other than reduction in the hourly rate of Mr. Tillotson. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-23-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID B. TILLOTSON of Flynn Riley Bailey & Pasek, LLP, Tel No. (415) 591-1150, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Deputy City Attorneys DAVID W. AMMONS and TIM FAMA of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415) 554-3800, appearing for Defendants City and County of San Francisco and Ellis-O'Farrell Parking Corporation.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELLIS-O'FARRELL PARKING CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Corporation breached the parties' listing agreement. Corporation admits at Request for Admission 44 that it never paid the commission and the evidence demonstrates that plaintiff performed its contractual obligation by obtaining a new tenant. Defendant's moving papers do not sufficiently raise the condition precedent issue and plaintiff may be able to recover a commission under Seck v. Foulks (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 556, 571. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
